name: Perth Food & Wine with Rottnest Island

days.desc: Assemble at Singapore Changi Airport for your flight to Perth, the state capital of Western Australia. Upon arrival, embark on a city sightseeing drive and drive past Parliament House. Later, make a photo stop at Kings Park and Botanic Garden. Later, make a photo stop at Blue Boat House, one of Perth city's insta and wedding photo hotspots for tourists and locals alike. Visit Cottesloe Beach before checking in at your hotel at Swan Valley. Meals: Meal On Board, Lunch, Dinner — Parliament House; Kings Park; Botanic Garden; Blue Boat House; Cottesloe Beach

This morning, begin your day at Caversham Wildlife Park, where you can explore and interact with native animals. Afterwards, enjoy a leisurely lunch paired with wine tasting at a local winery. Continue to the Margaret River Chocolate Company for the chance to sample and purchase locally made chocolates. Next, visit the Mondo Nougat Factory to observe the nougat-making and packaging process through a glass window, with the option to buy some to take home. Stop by Morish Nuts, renowned for its multi-award-winning handcrafted confectionery and premium Australian nuts. Finally, visit The House of Honey, a distinctive honey farm nestled in the heart of the Swan Valley. At the end of the day, head to Mandurah, a charming coastal city just south of Perth, known for its relaxed waterside lifestyle. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner — Caversham Wildlife Park; Local Winery; Margaret River Chocolate Company; Mondo Nougat Factory; Morish Nuts; The House of Honey

This morning, set off on a little adventure to discover one of the Giants of Mandurah. These enchanting, larger-than-life wooden sculptures by Danish artist Thomas Dambo are made entirely from recycled materials and hidden throughout the Mandurah region. Later, embark on a 3.5-hour Wild Seafood Experience with Mandurah Cruises, where you can enjoy the freshest seafood straight from the ocean. Feast on an 8-course seafood lunch, with drinks included, and experience the magic of "ocean to plate" dining – nothing tastes better than seafood caught and cooked just for you! Meals: Breakfast, Lunch — Giants of Mandurah; 3.5-hour Wild Seafood Experience with Mandurah Cruises

After breakfast, set off for Busselton and step onto the 140-year-old Busselton Jetty, the longest wooden structure in the Southern Hemisphere. After soaking up the sights and snapping plenty of photos, continue your journey back to Perth. Along the way, stop to take photos at Elizabeth Quay and the Bell Tower. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner — Busselton Jetty; Elizabeth Quay; Bell Tower

Today, set off on a full-day adventure to Rottnest Island, a protected nature haven famous for its friendly quokkas. Pass by the iconic Wadjemup Lighthouse, marvel at Henrietta Rocks, explore the Oliver Hill Gun Fortification and Tunnels, and soak in stunning vistas from the Mabel Cove lookout, before finishing the tour with a delightful historic train ride. Along the way, uncover the island's rich cultural and historical stories, from its maritime and military past to its bright future as one of Western Australia's most cherished destinations. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch — Rottnest Island; Wadjemup Lighthouse; Henrietta Rocks; Oliver Hill Gun Fortification and Tunnels; Mabel Cove lookout

Spend the day at leisure to explore the city of Perth. Shopping enthusiasts can browse the boutiques and stores at Hay Street Mall and Murray Street Mall. Alternatively, you may choose to join an optional tour to the Pinnacles, featuring a stunning sunset and stargazing experience. Meals: Breakfast

If time permits, you can do some last–minute shopping before you transfer to the airport for your flight home. Note: Travellers taking an early morning flight will be provided with a packed breakfast. Meals: Meal On Board, Breakfast

days.location: Board The Indian Pacific In Perth
days.desc: Your transcontinental adventure begins in Perth, where you'll gather with fellow travellers for a warm welcome and a celebratory start to your journey. As the Indian Pacific readies to depart, anticipation builds for the days ahead – crossing the country from ocean to ocean, along the world's longest stretch of straight railway track. With the sun dipping low over the west, settle into your cabin, meet new friends in the lounge, and savour your first dinner on board.

Awake to see the sunrise across the vast Nullarbor before arriving in Kalgoorlie, Australia's largest outback town. This morning, delve into the region's fascinating past and present with a visit to its gold-rush heritage and the modern-day Super Pit mine. Choose your Off Train Experience to explore Kalgoorlie in your own way, with morning refreshments included. Reboarding mid-morning for brunch, spend the afternoon at leisure on board. Relax with fine hospitality as the endless sweep of the Nullarbor passes by. Crossing borders and time zones, you'll arrive late evening in the near-ghost town of Cook, South Australia. Once bustling, today only a handful of residents remain, yet its stories endure. Step into this outback outpost to explore the ghost-like town, and as evening falls look skyward – here, under a canopy of stars unspoiled by light, you'll join a guided stargazing experience to spot the Southern Cross and other constellations that shine bright with dazzling clarity. Later, return to the train for a regionally inspired dinner and the gentle rhythm of the rails as you continue eastward.

days.location: Indian Pacific, Adelaide And Monarto Safari Lodge
days.desc: Spend the morning travelling through the pastoral heart of South Australia, with sweeping views of the Flinders Ranges, Spencer Gulf, and the Adelaide Plains unfolding beyond your window. A leisurely breakfast and lunch are served on board, inviting you to savour the relaxed rhythm of train travel. Mid-afternoon, the train arrives in Adelaide where you will be transferred by private vehicle to Monarto Safari Lodge, approximately 1 hour from the city centre and nestled in South Australia's expansive Murraylands. Monarto Safari Lodge offers an extraordinary escape into nature's embrace, combining luxurious Safari Tents with authentic experiences, during your stay you'll be surrounded by Africa's wildest animals. With some free time before checking in, you may choose to laze by the Lodge plunge pools, enjoy a game of pickle ball or indulge in the luxurious day spa at Monarto Safari Resort. Settle into your luxurious Safari Tent and enjoy the views as far as the eye can see. This evening dine at the Lodge delighting in a three course menu carefully curated by the onsite chef.

This morning breakfast is served in the Lodge before heading out to explore Monarto Safari Park via the guided Zu-Loop bus experience, or a combination of the Zu-Loop bus and walking. Monarto is the largest open-range safari experience in the world outside of Africa and spans more than 1,500 hectares and is home to more than 50 species of exotic and native mammals, birds and reptiles. You can hop on and off the bus at the wildlife viewing platforms and spend time observing your favourite animal species. Additionally, you can enhance your visit by attending Daily Keeper Presentations and booking a range of intimate behind-the-scenes animal experiences. Early evening, join the Sunset Wildlife Safari, soaking in the last rays of sun on your small group guided tour. Enjoy exclusive access to Monarto Safari Park's Wild Africa area after hours with ample opportunity to immerse yourself in the vast parklands and watch the free-roaming herds of African animals across 550 hectares. As you explore the lands from the comfort of a custom built Safari Landcruiser, you'll have the opportunity to observe and photograph animals including Southern White Rhinos, Giraffes, Cheetahs, Eland, Ostriches, and Zebras. Light refreshments will be served during the tour, capping off the unforgettable experience. Tonight you may choose to dine at the Lodge or in the Resort restaurant, Kutjera.
Today, your wild Monarto Luxury Stay comes to an end. After breakfast, check out of your accommodation and transfer by private vehicle to your choice of the Adelaide Airport or Adelaide CBD Hotel.

Jumping on a direct flight from Perth will get you to Broome in just two and a half hours, and flights depart daily. Regular direct flights to Broome are also available from Darwin, Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane. Soak up the beauty of Broome’s world-famous Cable Beach / Walmanyjun – a 22-kilometre stretch of pristine white sand and clear turquoise waters – and end your first day watching one of the most unforgettable ocean sunsets from a Cable Beach camel train. As dusk approaches, prepare to witness the unforgettable Staircase to the Moon. This is a natural phenomenon that occurs when the full moon rises over the tidal flats of Roebuck Bay / Yawuru Nagulagun between March and October. Leave Broome on the Great Northern Highway, for a four-and-a-half-hour drive across to Fitzroy Crossing. Explore this true outback town and try your luck at Barramundi fishing on one of the largest rivers on Earth. Choose between two properties offering motel and caravan and camping options for your overnight stay.

days.location: Fitzroy Crossing to Geikie Gorge return
days.desc: Just 20 kilometres north of town lies Geikie Gorge National Park / Danggu. Fitzroy River’s floodwaters carved the 30-meter-deep gorge through Geikie Range. Discover fossils, steep rock walls, fish, crocodiles, and stingrays. Swim off sandbanks, and spot river gum trees, mangroves, figs, bats, and diverse birdlife. Or you can go bush walking along two dedicated trails Make your way back to Fitzroy Crossing for the night.

Welcome on board with FIESTA VACATIONS!! On arrival in Sydney, You will be met by our local representative and privately transferred to your hotel. On arrival check-In and relax. Later Proceed to the city tour which includes: Bondi Beach, Sydney Aquarium, Harbour Bridge, Opera House, Centre Point Sydney tower, China town Sydney Etc Dinner and Overnight stay at Sydney — Bondi Beach; Sydney Aquarium; Harbour Bridge; Opera House; Centre Point Sydney tower; China town Sydney Etc Dinner; Later Proceed; Sydney Etc Dinner

Escape the hustle and bustle of Sydney and proceed to the Blue Mountains. Enjoy the stunning natural beauty of this World Heritage-listed parkland. Walk through lush rainforest, hike along bush tracks and travel over escarpments to reveal unparalleled views of the countryside! (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Sydney — Blue Mountain

Today after a breakfast, Check-out and proceed to airport to board your flight to Brisbane. Upon arrival Meet our representative where in you will be transferred to Gold coast. Check in to the hotel and relax for the day. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Sydney

Today after breakfast, Proceed to enjoy Movie World: Warner Bros. Movie World is a popular movie related theme park on the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia. It is owned and operated by Village Roadshow since the take over from Time Warner and is the only movie related park in Australia. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Gold Coast — Movie World; Warner Bros; Village Roadshow; Time Warner

Today after breakfast, Proceed to enjoy Sea World is a marine mammal park, oceanarium, and theme park located on the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia. It includes rides, animal exhibits and other attractions, and promotes conservation through education and through the rescue and rehabilitation of sick, injured or orphaned wildlife. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Gold Coast — Sea World

Today after breakfast, Check-out from the hotel and proceed to airport to board your flight to Cairns and Upon arrival you will be transferred to Hotel. check in and relax. Evening proceed to Aboriginal and Tjapukai Night Tour: Come and join the traditional people of Australia in a special evening of Aboriginal culture. Tjapukai means rainforest and on this engaging cultural tour you'll hear the story of the Tjapukai people, watch theatrical performances, join in interactive activities and learn the traditonal customs of the rainforest and its people. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Cairns — Japukai By Night; Tjapukai Night Tour

Today after breakfast, Proceed to One of Australia's most remarkable natural gifts, the Great Barrier Reef is blessed with the breathtaking beauty of the world's largest coral reef. The reef contains an abundance of marine life and comprises of over 3000 individual reef systems and coral cays and literally hundreds of picturesque tropical islands with some of the worlds most beautiful sun-soaked, golden beaches. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Cairns — Great Barrier Reef

Today after breakfast, Check-out from the hotel and proceed to airport to board your flight to Melbourne. Upon Arrival you will be transferred to Hotel, Check-In & relax. Melbourne is one of the world's most liveable cities and this Melbourne City Tour will show you why. Enjoy the rich cultural vibe, the relaxed elegant charm and the cosmopolitan feel that Melbourne has to offer. This Melbourne Tour will allow you to explore architectual treasures, beautiful historical gardens and the thriving restaurants, cafes and markets for excellent value for money. Tours include Queen Victoria Market, Chinatown, Botanical Gardens, Albert Park Lake, St Patrick's Cathedral and trendy Southbank on the river. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Melbourne — Queen Victoria Market; Chinatown; Botanical Gardens; Albert Park Lake; St Patrick's Cathedral; Melbourne City Tour

Todat after breakfast, Proceed to The Great Ocean Road winds through varying terrain as it hugs the coastline of South West Victoria, spanning 243kms. Let someone else do the driving as you focus on the breathtaking scenery at this most popular tourist destination. Our Great Ocean Road Tours include stops at beautiful untouched beaches, lush ancient rainforests, rugged limestone cliffs and quaint seaside towns and is one of the most popular Melbourne tours. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Melbourne — Great Ocean Road Drive

Today after breakfast, Proceed to Only 90 minutes from Melbourne and one of our most unique Melbourne tours, Phillip Island features spectacular coastal scenery, charming heritage and an abundance of Australian wildlife. Visit a wildlife park, the Koala Conservation Centre, the Nobbies Centre, the Chocolate Factory, the Phillip Island Grand Prix Circuit and much much more. Not to be missed is the main event - The Penguin Parade. See the world's smallest penguins in their natural habitat from viewing stands and boardwalks as they waddle up the beach to the safety of their homes in the sand dunes. This magical penguin procession, and all stops along the way on the Phillip Island Tours are treats that will never be forgotten. (Breakfast, Lunch and Dinner) Overnight stay at Melbourne — Phillip Island; Koala Conservation Centre; Nobbies Centre; Chocolate Factory

Today after breakfast, Check-out and the Day at leisure until you transfer to the airport for your departure flight. Good times need never end! As you get ready for the airport and your flight back home, re-live the high points of your Fiesta Vacations tour.We are confident you will join us again, sometime soon. ****** TOUR ENDS******

As you get ready for the airport and your flight back home, re-live the high points of your Fiesta Vacations tour.We are confident you will join us again, sometime soon. ****** TOUR ENDS******
Touching down in sunny Sydney, we will meet you at our centrally located hostel to settle in. You'll then meet up with your travel crew and get excited for the adventures to come.

Today we'll show you around the beautiful city of Sydney and learn about it's fascinating culture and history. This evening we'll head out for dinner and a few drinks and show you the best of Sydney's nightlife!

Today we visit the world famous Bondi Beach where you can take a swim or just relax and work on your tan. We'll then do the famous Bondi to Coogee Coastal walk and enjoy an Aussie beach BBQ. Later, we'll jump on our overnight bus to Byron Bay.
Spend the day exploring the beautiful and famous surf town of Byron Bay. We'll enjoy a night out this evening.
Today you'll enjoy a relaxing yoga class before you learn to surf with real surfer dudes. The proper Byron Bay experience!
This morning we'll wake for sunrise at the iconic Cape Byron Lighthouse. We'll take in the stunning views and enjoy our last day in Byron.
Today we arrive in Surfers Paradise. Explore the city and then work on your tan on one of the seemingly endless white sand beaches of this famous piece of Aussie coastline.

This morning we'll be heading out to go jet boating along the iconic Surfers coastline. The rest of the day is up to you - Spend the day on the beach or opt to visit one of the many famous theme parks on the Gold Coast. Meet up for a night out together!

We head up to Brisbane today and will get to explore the highlights of Queensland's capital with a city orientation tour. We'll then get to chill out at the only city man-made beach in Australia called ‘The Lagoon.'
Today is yours to explore Brisbane, even visit the iconic Australia Zoo made famous by Steve Irwin. Tonight we'll enjoy a night out in Fortitude Valley.

Heading up to the laidback coastal town of Rainbow Beach, this morning we'll get to soak in the relaxed vibes that Rainbow Beach has to offer. Kick back on the sand or take a stroll through town. Later we'll help you with all of your onward travel in Oz and beyond!

Head over to K'gari for the next 3 days where you'll get to drive 4x4's around the biggest sand island in the world. The beach and sand dunes are your highway for the next 3 days and then sleep beneath the stars in a purpose built campsite with your group. Small Change: Explore K'gari with Eco certified operator Dingos working closely with native Butchulla representatives to follow cultural protocols.
On your second day on K'gari, swim in the incredible turquoise waters of Lake McKenzie and float down the lazy river of Eli Creek.

On your final day on K'gari, explore more interesting spots on the island like the Maheno Shipwreck, chill in the bubbles of the Champagne Pools, learn about the Aboriginal history on the island and spot dingoes in the wild.
Arriving back in Rainbow Beach, we'll explore the colourful sand dunes before umping on the overnight bus to the Whitsundays.

Made up of 74 tropical islands, we'll board our boat for the next 2 nights. Relax and sunbathe on the deck as we sail around the islands. Small Change: Sail the stunning Whitsundays with Eco Certified operator Prosail, where sustainability meets adventure.

On your second day sailing around the Whitsundays you'll snorkel with an abundance of marine life, then later you'll get to explore the amazing swirls of fine white sand on the world-famous Whitehaven Beach.

On your final day sailing the Whitsundays islands, you will continue to explore the stunning islands, pristine beaches, and vibrant marine life on your way back to the mainland. Once in Airlie Beach, you'll have some free time.
Today you'll have some free time to explore Airlie Beach, or try out some epic optional activities.
Today we'll jump on the ferry to a slice of tropical paradise - Magnetic Island, to explore the hidden beaches.
Today we'll hike up the historic Forts Walk. Taking in the stunning views, keep your eyes open because if you're lucky you'll even spot wild koalas and rock wallabies!

The chilled out beachfront town of Mission Beach is our next relaxation location. It's also filled with adrenaline pumping activities that you may choose to do, from skydiving at 15,000ft over the Great Barrier Reef and rainforests, or white water rafting on the Tully River Rapids through the World Heritage Rainforest.
Today's activities are up to you! Get that skydive in or relax on the beach. We'll then meet up for a BBQ together!
Welcome to the ultimate backpacker adventure hub, Cairns.

Today you'll tick off a massive bucket list item and experience a full day on the Great Barrier Reef. You'll spend the day sailing and snorkelling unique outer reef locations in this World Heritage listed site. Once you're off the boat we'll get ready for a fun night out! Small Change: Discover the Great Barrier Reef with Passions of Paradise, a locally owned Eco Certified operator dedicated to sustainability and marine conservation.
Today is yours to explore Cairns! Opt for a heart-pumping bungee jump or white water rafting.

Exploring the Aussie oasis of the Atherton Tablelands is next for Cairns, where you will swim under waterfalls and dormant volcano craters, and slide down natural rock slides, all while learning about this extraordinary place from your experienced guide. The night will finish with a farewell dinner and drinks to toast to the incredible adventures and friends we have made.
Your epic Aussie adventure ends in Cairns on the morning of day 28, but we are here to help with onward travel in Oz for the rest of your time in Australia.
